title: The image that speaks
why.no-alt: The image is mute. Run it and look — it does not load, and where it stood there is nothing. Half your listing vanished and nobody knew.
why.empty-alt: You wrote alt="" — empty. That makes sense ONLY if the image is pure decoration. This one is the thing you are selling.
why.useless-alt: You wrote "image" or "photo". A blind person hears: "image: image". You told them there is a picture — they already knew that. You did not tell them what is on it.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
blocks.0.text: Run the page on the right. The image does not load — I put the wrong path in on purpose.
blocks.1.text: Look at what is left: nothing. A hole. Your listing says "here is a photo of the bike", and then — a gap. Half the listing vanished and nobody knows what it was.
blocks.4.text: alt is what you see when the image cannot be seen. It comes from "alternative" — replacement text.
blocks.5.text: Add it and run. Now, where the broken image was, it says what should have been there. The listing is whole again.
blocks.6.text: But it is not only for broken images
blocks.7.text: A blind person sees no images. Ever. Their computer reads the page aloud — and when it reaches an , it reads the alt.
blocks.8.text: If there is no alt, they hear: "image". That is all. Or, with bad luck — the file name: "img underscore 2847 dot jpg".
blocks.9.text: For them, your image IS that text. There is no other.
blocks.11.items.0: Describe what IS SEEN, not what the file is. "Blue mountain bike, side view", not "photo".
blocks.11.items.1: Do not write "photo of…" — they already know it is a photo. Their computer told them.
blocks.11.items.2: Short. One sentence. Not a novel.
blocks.12.text: The test: close your eyes and imagine someone reading the page aloud to you. Does your alt tell you what you need?
blocks.14.text: There is one case: alt="" — deliberately empty. Used when the image is pure decoration and carries no meaning. A decorative rule, a background, an icon next to text that already says the same thing.
blocks.15.text: Then the empty alt tells the reader: "skip me, there is nothing here for you". That is better than a bad alt.
blocks.16.text: An empty alt — on purpose. A missing alt — forgotten. The difference is enormous.
blocks.18.text: Google does not see images. It reads the alt. If you are selling a bike and your alt says "img_2847", you are invisible to search.
blocks.19.text: And: images load slowly on a weak connection. While it is missing, the person reads the alt and knows what is coming.
blocks.20.text: One attribute. Four people use it — the blind person, Google, the person on a weak connection, and you, when something breaks.
blocks.21.text: That is why alt is not optional.
blocks.22.text: Your turn. Fix the image on the right — give it a voice.
